title: Towards Sulphite-Free Winemaking: A New Horizon of Vinification and Maturation
author: MAcaluso, Monica
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This article presents an innovative sulphite-free winemaking protocol that replaces traditional SO₂ preservation with compounds derived from wine production chain waste (grape pomace tannins and anthocyanins). Developed under TRACEWINDU’s Work Package 3, the protocol was piloted at La Cura winery and demonstrated comparable or superior phenolic stability and volatile profiles to traditionally vinified wines, establishing a circular economy approach to winemaking with health-related valorisation potential.
steps_raw: This article presents an innovative sulphite-free winemaking protocol that replaces traditional SO₂ preservation with compounds derived from wine production chain waste (grape pomace tannins and anthocyanins). Developed under TRACEWINDU’s Work Package 3, the protocol was piloted at La Cura winery and demonstrated comparable or superior phenolic stability and volatile profiles to traditionally vinified wines, establishing a circular economy approach to winemaking with health-related valorisation potential.
keywords: sulphite-free winemaking; SO2 replacement; grape pomace; circular economy; phenolic stability; innovative vinification; wine quality
